summary: In this keynote lecture, Robert Langer recounts his unconventional career journey from chemical engineering to pioneering drug delivery and tissue engineering. He highlights the immense skepticism he faced from the scientific establishment, the decades-long timeline required for medical translation, and the necessity of perseverance and collaboration in bringing life-saving technologies to the world.

principles.statement: Perseverance is required to overcome scientific consensus.
principles.rationale: Langer faced years of grant rejections, job rejections, and direct criticism from senior scientists who believed his ideas were impossible, but he succeeded by refusing to give up on his research or his patents.
principles.source_quote: I don't like to quit and I started to think how could we convince the patent examiner to allow this patent

principles.statement: Medical translation is a decades-long process.
principles.rationale: It took nearly three decades from his initial discovery of angiogenesis inhibitors to the first FDA-approved drug utilizing the mechanism.
principles.source_quote: medicine doesn't happen quickly the translational part. So, so we published that in 1976. It took another 28 years... before the first blood vessel inhibitor would get approved.

principles.statement: Meaningful impact is the result of collaboration.
principles.rationale: He attributes the widespread success and real-world application of his lab's discoveries to the many people he worked with at MIT and beyond.
principles.source_quote: anything that you do, you know, which really ends up having these effects is the work of many people.

frameworks.name: Overcoming Patent Rejections via Citation Search
frameworks.when_to_apply: When a patent examiner rejects an invention because they believe it violates scientific principles or is obvious.
steps: Perform a science citation search on your foundational paper.
Find quotes from top experts in the field stating your results were 'surprising' or demonstrated the opposite of prior expectations.
Obtain written affidavits from those experts confirming they wrote the statements.
Present the affidavits to the patent examiner to prove the invention's novelty and non-obviousness.
frameworks.source_quote: I wondered maybe somebody wrote that down. So you can do something called a science citation search, meaning you could look back at who at your nature paper and see who wrote about it.

mental_models.name: Walking through a wall
mental_models.description: A metaphor representing the prevailing scientific dogma of the 1970s, which assumed that large molecules could not diffuse through solid polymers. Langer had to break this mental model to invent advanced drug delivery systems.
mental_models.example: they all said to him that you really aren't going to be able to do it because they can't get through solid materials anymore than like we could walk through a wall.

signature_quotes.text: I actually found over 200 different ways to get this to not work.
signature_quotes.context: Describing his two years of trial and error to create the first advanced drug delivery system for large molecules.
Explaining his reaction when the hospital's tech transfer office told him to give up on his patent after five years of rejections.

signature_quotes.text: medicine doesn't happen quickly the translational part.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the 28-year gap between his initial publication and the first FDA approval of a blood vessel inhibitor.

signature_quotes.text: anything that you do, you know, which really ends up having these effects is the work of many people.
signature_quotes.context: Concluding his talk by emphasizing the importance of collaboration in achieving scientific impact.

anti_patterns.description: Relying entirely on large corporations to translate your research into real-world treatments.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Large companies may license a patent, but if early experiments don't turn out exactly as they want, they often give up. Starting your own company is sometimes necessary to push the technology forward.

anti_patterns.description: Quitting when the scientific consensus tells you your idea is impossible.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Experts often base their skepticism on existing paradigms. If Langer had listened to the Nobel laureates or the senior scientists who told him to find another job, advanced drug delivery wouldn't exist.
